candidate_problems.text: Specify a concrete representation for explanatory objects E=(O,R,P,C) and test whether systems searching that space can generate structures satisfying constraints not explicitly instantiated in training data.

Determine whether remaining in the image of the language map L:E->text is sufficient to block genuine novelty, or whether novel explanatory structure can be implicitly realized without explicit nonlinguistic search over E.
Define and operationalize Q(E) so that explanatory adequacy, invariance, novelty, and criticizability can be jointly evaluated without collapsing into surface plausibility.
Identify transformations on explanatory graphs that preserve deep constraints while varying assumptions, and test whether current LLM-based systems can reliably traverse them.
